action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 7 scored 0.6118 (best so far) using directional utility aggregation with Dijkstra distances. Iteration 8 tried a different variant and crashed to 0.3076, likely due to a bug or poor fallback. Analysis: I don't have the complete iteration 7 code (truncated in context), but I know directional aggregation can work. I will rewrite predict.py from scratch with a robust implementation: directional aggregation, conservative parameters (d+3.6 denominator, moderate visited/history penalties), anti-backtracking momentum, step-budget guard, and a careful multi-tier fallback (frontiers -> unvisited nodes -> history-avoidance). Decision: Write complete predict.py and train.py, run smoke test, then immediately submit evaluation to measure score and iteration time.
